heading: Omitted
label: 2515 to 2525 Omitted
notes: CodificationSections 2515 to 2525 terminated as of , pursuant to .Aug. 31, 1965section 2525 of this titleSection 2515, , , , set forth penalties for violations of this chapter.Pub. L. 87–27, § 1875 Stat. 60May 1, 1961Section 2516, , , , provided for employment of expediters and administrative employees.Pub. L. 87–27, § 1975 Stat. 61May 1, 1961Section 2517, , , , provided that Secretary of Commerce maintain as a permanent record a list of applications approved for financial assistance and for public inspection thereof.Pub. L. 87–27, § 2075 Stat. 61May 1, 1961Section 2518, , , , provided for labor standards for laborers and mechanics; their rate of wages and overtime and for enforcement thereof.Pub. L. 87–27, § 2175 Stat. 61May 1, 1961Section 2519, , , , provided for an annual report to Congress and for contents thereof.Pub. L. 87–27, § 2275 Stat. 62May 1, 1961Section 2520, , , , authorized appropriations necessary to carry out provisions of this chapter.Pub. L. 87–27, § 2375 Stat. 62May 1, 1961Section 2521, , , ; , , , provided for use of other available services and facilities of other agencies and instrumentalities of Federal Government and for rules and regulations governing said use.Pub. L. 87–27, § 2475 Stat. 62Pub. L. 87–793, title II, § 607(a)(4)76 Stat. 849May 1, 1961Oct. 11, 1962Section 2522, , , , stated that each recipient of assistance under this chapter shall keep such records as Secretary shall prescribe and provided for audit of such records by Federal Government.Pub. L. 87–27, § 2575 Stat. 63May 1, 1961Section 2523, , , , provided that Secretary shall establish and conduct a continuing program of study and research and shall include in his annual report to Congress his findings resulting therefrom and his recommendations for legislative and other action.Pub. L. 87–27, § 2775 Stat. 63May 1, 1961Section 2524, , , , defined “State”, “States”, and “United States” as used in this chapter.Pub. L. 87–27, § 2875 Stat. 63May 1, 1961Section 2525, , , ; , , , stated that termination date of this chapter and all authority conferred thereunder shall be , and that Secretary of the Treasury shall be responsible for liquidation of affairs and functions conducted under this chapter.Pub. L. 87–27, § 2975 Stat. 63Pub. L. 89–5579 Stat. 195May 1, 1961June 30, 1965Aug. 31, 1965
